Okay, so you know the basics, that it's an auditing and reporting and compliance and security tool, and it helps you secure mainly five data sources, such as SharePoint Online, Windows File Servers, Active Directory, Entry ID, Exchange Online, and partially SQL Server.
This is the main opening dashboard of OneSecure.
It's a multi-tenant dashboard.
Which means that if you have multiple tenants, multiple locations, or if you are a managed service provider and you have multiple clients, you may have seen all of them on the same dashboard.
This dashboard provides you the most critical information about your tenants, the top alerts, the top organizations with most alerts, and the top organizations with the highest risks.

Risk Assessment Dashboard is one of the best functions of the product.

is available in multiple versions, so the risk assessment has a standard and an advanced version as well.

Auditor is mainly, primarily a compliance tool, and security is only a secondary use case.

There is no such feature in OneSecurity yet.

It's still very important, but the ratio of compliance and security use cases is much more balanced at OneSecure than at Auditor.

They're using ChatGPT or something like that.

Like, it's really core, like, I would say, even in whether it's Auditor, Access Analyzer, our ITDR stuff, our directory security stuff, a lot of that revolves around our credentials in Active Directory.
We just have a lot of experience in Active Directory, and that's kind of like the control plan for a lot of companies.

So if you, you know, so the bottom half of this is identity, the top half is data.
So you definitely Microsoft, but if they're Microsoft only, they can maybe squeak by with just Microsoft Purview and some of the other Microsoft tools.
So the heterogeneity is also sort of important because Microsoft doesn't really do much for those other data sources.
So, you know, the problem our customers are having is data is everywhere.
They don't know what they don't know in terms of what's sensitive.
Even in interesting use cases, stale data costs them a lot of money and they don't even know what data they could get rid of, right?
So that's one problem.
And then identity sprawl is another problem, right?
So they have not just human identities, joiners, movers, leavers, and governing who's coming in, who's going out, but also now more and more machine or non-human identities.
So service accounts, you know, software applications.
And now Agenda AI or AI agents all have identities too.
And basically the challenge you have historically is that data security tools and identity security tools are two different universes.
And so people like Varonis focuses on securing, you know, scanning your data sources, identifying sensitive data, helping you lock that down.
There's encryption tools.
There's all these kind of things.
However, we know that attackers more and more are logging in, not breaking in.
And they're logging in because they compromise people's identities.
They compromise people's credentials.
They escalate privileges.
They get admin privileges.
And then they can walk over and log in to the sensitive data stores and steal the data, right?
So it's not enough to just try to secure your data.
You also have to secure, you have to.
Know what identity, who has access to what data, should they or shouldn't they have access.

As people are joining, moving, and leaving companies, as you're adding software agents that have access to data, you need a way to govern all those identities, and you need to know who has access to what data, and should they or shouldn't they.
How are you governing the identities and their access to the data?
How are you protecting the identities so that if you get breached, they can't also steal your data?
And this is where the industry as a whole has fallen down, right?
Because you've got SailPoint and Savient and all these identity security providers.
You have the data security providers, and there are very few like NetRex that bring the two disciplines together.
And so if you kind of then hit the next build, this is really what we're doing is bringing data security and identity security together.
And so allowing you to, you know, kind of discover what you have in your environments, secure and harden.
on both the data and identity side, govern, so governance is key.
Like, it's one thing to scan and say, these people have access, so we need to lock them down, but what about tomorrow?
What about the day after tomorrow when people change in your environment and someone, you know, who has, who can give, who can grant privileges, all that kind of stuff?
Then we, we do monitoring and, and, and documentation of, of, of proof for compliance purposes, obviously all the auditing stuff that Donnie was showing you.
And then we also have recovery more on the directory side.
We don't, we're not like Rubrik that does it on the data store side, but we do it for your, we do it for your, your directories, right?

For AD, Entra, and Okta, so if you, whether it's a ransomware attack that takes down your, your directories, or whether it's a user error or something like that, to help you fully recover your directories.
So that's kind of, at a high level, we have this.

And then on.
On the identity side, right, these are also categories.

You have SailPoint and Semperis and Savient that compete in IGA.

The six solutions that are out there were built earlier this year.
We're kind of like, hey, how do we take these 18 products that we've acquired?
Why?

Customers would sort of start picking them apart, saying, well, I don't need this product.
